• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

PhillyStat360 — 05 v3: Model Output Analysis (Probability-Only)

MUSA Practicum Team

2026-04-20

Changes from v2 (05_output_analysis_v2.Rmd):

Issue Location Fix
Binary flag analysis invalid (ROSE threshold 3.33e-05 ≠ real-world threshold) All sections Removed all rf_flag / flag_rate / precision metrics; use rf_prob only
Density plot filtered on invalid rf_flag Section 2b Changed to rf_prob > 0 (no-tree-vote exclusion, threshold-free)
Tier breaks inconsistent with Tim’s 5-tier standard Section 2c Changed to equal-width 0.2 steps: 0–20%, 20–40%, 40–60%, 60–80%, 80–100%
Spatial map empty (PWD_PARCELS.geojson = 421 MB, fails to load) Section 3c + 4 Replaced with tigris ZCTA zip-code boundaries (~few MB)

Why no binary flags? rf_flag was derived from threshold = 3.33e-05, which was optimised by ROSE on an oversampled training set (50/50 balance). Applied to the real-world data (1.1% positive rate) it flags ~7.4% of parcels — far above the true vacancy rate. The 04a script notes: “binary flag analysis is invalid; use probabilities only.” All summaries below use predicted probabilities.

0. Setup

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(leaflet)
library(htmlwidgets)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

.root      <- if (dir.exists("PhillyStat360")) "PhillyStat360" else ".."
feat_path  <- paste0(.root, "/data/")
raw_path   <- paste0(.root, "/rawdata/")
graph_path <- paste0(.root, "/graphs/")
out_path   <- "F:/PhillyStat360_targets/data/"
dir.create(graph_path, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(out_path,   showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

1. Load Predictions & Features

# tar_read() when running via tar_make(); falls back to CSV for standalone knit.
# tar_render() scans for these tar_read() calls to auto-declare dependencies on
# predictions_data and features_data — so this file re-runs automatically
# whenever any upstream target changes.
preds_df <- tryCatch(
  targets::tar_read(predictions_data),
  error = function(e)
    read_csv(paste0(feat_path, "all_predictions_rf.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
)

features_df <- tryCatch(
  targets::tar_read(features_data),
  error = function(e)
    read_csv(paste0(feat_path, "features_residential.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
)

# category_code / category_code_description live in ovs_residential.csv, not features
ovs_meta <- read_csv(paste0(feat_path, "ovs_residential.csv"),
                     show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  select(parcel_number,
         category_code, category_code_description,
         zip_code, census_tract, zoning)

# Join predictions → features → category metadata
analysis_df <- preds_df %>%
  left_join(
    features_df %>%
      select(parcel_number,
             exterior_condition, building_age, log_livable_area),
    by = "parcel_number"
  ) %>%
  left_join(ovs_meta, by = "parcel_number")

cat("analysis_df:", comma(nrow(analysis_df)), "parcels\n")
## analysis_df: 520,196 parcels
cat("OVS=1 (observed):", comma(sum(analysis_df$ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
## OVS=1 (observed): 5,934
cat("rf_prob > 0:", comma(sum(analysis_df$rf_prob > 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    "(", percent(mean(analysis_df$rf_prob > 0, na.rm = TRUE), 0.1), ")\n")
## rf_prob > 0: 14,880 ( 2.9% )
cat("category_code_description available:",
    comma(sum(!is.na(analysis_df$category_code_description))), "parcels\n")
## category_code_description available: 520,196 parcels

2. Looking Across Types with Probability

2a. Distribution by OPA Category Code

type_summary <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(category_code_description)) %>%
  group_by(category_code_description) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels        = n(),
    n_observed_vac   = sum(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    obs_rate         = mean(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pred_prob   = mean(rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_pred_prob = median(rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_pred_prob))

type_summary %>%
  mutate(
    n_parcels        = comma(n_parcels),
    n_observed_vac   = comma(n_observed_vac),
    obs_rate         = percent(obs_rate,         0.1),
    mean_pred_prob   = percent(mean_pred_prob,   0.1),
    median_pred_prob = percent(median_pred_prob, 0.1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Category", "N Parcels", "N Observed Vacant",
                  "Obs Rate", "Mean P(vacant)", "Median P(vacant)"),
    caption   = "Model Output by OPA Property Category — RF model (probabilities only)"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#fde8e8")
Model Output by OPA Property Category — RF model (probabilities only)
Category N Parcels N Observed Vacant Obs Rate Mean P(vacant) Median P(vacant)
MIXED USE 14,021 344 2.5% 0.8% 0.0%
MULTI FAMILY 40,892 603 1.5% 0.6% 0.0%
SINGLE FAMILY 461,662 4,956 1.1% 0.4% 0.0%
APARTMENTS > 4 UNITS 3,621 31 0.9% 0.3% 0.0%

2b. Probability Distribution by Category

major_categories <- type_summary %>%
  filter(n_parcels >= 500) %>%
  pull(category_code_description)

# ~75% of parcels have rf_prob = 0 (no tree in the RF voted positive).
# Excluding those zeros reveals the meaningful variation in the distribution.
# Filter: rf_prob > 0 (threshold-free — just excludes true RF zero-votes)
nonzero_df <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(category_code_description %in% major_categories,
         rf_prob > 0)

n_total_maj   <- analysis_df %>% filter(category_code_description %in% major_categories) %>% nrow()
n_nonzero_maj <- nrow(nonzero_df)

p_density <- nonzero_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rf_prob, fill = category_code_description,
             color = category_code_description)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.25, adjust = 1.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.40),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  labs(
    title    = "P(vacant) Distribution by OPA Category (Non-Zero Predictions Only)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Showing parcels with rf_prob > 0: ",
      comma(n_nonzero_maj), " of ", comma(n_total_maj),
      " (", percent(n_nonzero_maj / n_total_maj, 0.1), ") | X-axis capped at 40%"
    ),
    x = "Predicted P(vacant)", y = "Density",
    fill = "Category", color = "Category"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_density

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "prob_distribution_by_category_v3.png"),
       plot = p_density, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)

2c. Mean P(vacant) & Five-Tier Distribution by Category

# ----- Chart A: Mean P(vacant) by category (probability-based) ---------------
mean_prob_cat <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(category_code_description %in% major_categories) %>%
  group_by(category_code_description) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_prob = mean(rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
    obs_rate  = mean(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_prob) %>%
  mutate(category_code_description = factor(category_code_description,
                                             levels = category_code_description))

p_mean_prob <- mean_prob_cat %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_prob, y = category_code_description)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2166ac", alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_point(aes(x = obs_rate), color = "#d73027", size = 3.5, shape = 18) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(mean_prob, 0.01)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
  labs(
    title    = "A. Mean Predicted P(vacant) by Property Category",
    subtitle = "Blue bar = mean RF predicted probability  |  Red diamond = observed OVS rate",
    x = "Probability / Rate", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# ----- Chart B: Distribution-aware 5-tier breakdown --------------------------
# rf_prob = 0 (no RF tree voted positive) is treated as its own tier — it is
# qualitatively different from a tiny-but-nonzero probability.
# Remaining breaks are log-spaced to match the actual right-skewed distribution.
tier_levels <- c("No Signal\n(= 0)",
                 "Low\n(0–5%)",
                 "Moderate\n(5–20%)",
                 "High\n(20–50%)",
                 "Very High\n(> 50%)")

tier_all <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(category_code_description %in% major_categories) %>%
  mutate(
    tier = case_when(
      rf_prob == 0                     ~ tier_levels[1],
      rf_prob <= 0.05                  ~ tier_levels[2],
      rf_prob <= 0.20                  ~ tier_levels[3],
      rf_prob <= 0.50                  ~ tier_levels[4],
      TRUE                             ~ tier_levels[5]
    ),
    tier = factor(tier, levels = tier_levels)
  ) %>%
  count(category_code_description, tier) %>%
  group_by(category_code_description) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(category_code_description = factor(category_code_description,
                                             levels = levels(mean_prob_cat$category_code_description)))

p_tiers_cat <- tier_all %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = pct, y = category_code_description, fill = tier)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = if_else(pct > 0.02, percent(pct, 1), "")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 3, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No Signal\n(= 0)"    = "#d9d9d9",
    "Low\n(0–5%)"         = "#74add1",
    "Moderate\n(5–20%)"   = "#fdae61",
    "High\n(20–50%)"      = "#f46d43",
    "Very High\n(> 50%)"  = "#d73027"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "B. Vacancy Risk Tier Distribution by Property Category",
    subtitle = "\"No Signal\" = RF assigned exactly 0 (no tree voted positive) | breaks reflect actual distribution",
    x = "% of Parcels", y = NULL, fill = "Vacancy Risk Tier"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_mean_prob / p_tiers_cat + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.2))

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "mean_prob_and_tiers_by_category_v3.png"),
       plot = p_mean_prob / p_tiers_cat + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.2)),
       width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

2d. Category Calibration: Predicted vs Observed

p_cat_calib <- type_summary %>%
  filter(n_parcels >= 200) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_pred_prob, y = obs_rate,
             label = category_code_description, size = n_parcels)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(0.1)) +
  scale_size_continuous(labels = comma, range = c(2, 8)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Category Calibration: Predicted vs Observed Vacancy Rate",
    subtitle = "Points above diagonal = model under-predicts for that category | RF model",
    x = "Mean Predicted P(vacant)", y = "Observed Vacancy Rate",
    size = "N Parcels"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

tryCatch({
  p_cat_calib <- p_cat_calib +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(size = 3.5, max.overlaps = 15)
  p_cat_calib
  ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "category_calibration_v3.png"),
         plot = p_cat_calib, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300)
}, error = function(e) {
  message("Install ggrepel for label repelling: install.packages('ggrepel')")
  p_cat_calib_nolabel <- p_cat_calib +
    ggplot2::geom_text(size = 3, vjust = -0.8)
  ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "category_calibration_v3.png"),
         plot = p_cat_calib_nolabel, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300)
})

3. Model Output Across Space & Categories

3a. Summary by Zip Code

zip_summary <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(zip_code)) %>%
  group_by(zip_code) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels      = n(),
    n_obs_vacant   = sum(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    obs_rate       = mean(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pred_prob = mean(rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_pred_prob))

zip_summary %>%
  head(20) %>%
  mutate(
    n_parcels      = comma(n_parcels),
    n_obs_vacant   = comma(n_obs_vacant),
    obs_rate       = percent(obs_rate,       0.1),
    mean_pred_prob = percent(mean_pred_prob, 0.1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Zip Code", "N Parcels", "N Obs. Vacant", "Obs. Rate",
                  "Mean P(vacant)"),
    caption   = "Top 20 Zip Codes by Mean Predicted P(vacant) — RF model"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1:5, background = "#fff3e0")

3b. Zip Code Bar Chart

p_zip_bar <- zip_summary %>%
  filter(n_parcels >= 100) %>%
  mutate(zip_code = fct_reorder(as.character(zip_code), mean_pred_prob)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_pred_prob, y = zip_code)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = mean_pred_prob)) +
  geom_point(aes(x = obs_rate), shape = 18, size = 3, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7fbff", high = "#08306b",
                      labels = percent_format(0.1),
                      name = "Mean\nP(vacant)") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(0.1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Model Output by Zip Code: Predicted Probability vs Observed Rate",
    subtitle = "Bars = mean predicted P(vacant) | Diamond = observed OVS=1 rate | RF model",
    x = "Rate / Probability", y = "Zip Code"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_zip_bar

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "model_output_by_zip_v3.png"),
       plot = p_zip_bar, width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300)

3c. Spatial Map: Predicted P(vacant) by Zip Code

# PWD_PARCELS.geojson is 421 MB and fails to load in knitting.
# Use tigris ZCTA boundaries (~few MB) instead — join to zip_summary aggregates.

phl_zips <- zip_summary %>%
  filter(n_parcels >= 50) %>%
  pull(zip_code) %>%
  as.character()

zcta_sf <- tryCatch({
  zctas(starts_with = "191", year = 2020, progress_bar = FALSE) %>%
    filter(ZCTA5CE20 %in% phl_zips) %>%
    left_join(
      zip_summary %>% mutate(zip_code = as.character(zip_code)),
      by = c("ZCTA5CE20" = "zip_code")
    )
}, error = function(e) {
  message("Could not download ZCTA boundaries: ", e$message)
  NULL
})

if (!is.null(zcta_sf) && nrow(zcta_sf) > 0) {
  p_map_static <- ggplot(zcta_sf) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_pred_prob), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
    scale_fill_gradient(
      low      = "#f7fbff",
      high     = "#08306b",
      labels   = percent_format(0.1),
      name     = "Mean\nP(vacant)",
      na.value = "gray90"
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = "Philadelphia Vacancy Risk: Mean Predicted P(vacant) by Zip Code",
      subtitle = paste0("RF model | n = ", comma(nrow(analysis_df)), " parcels"),
      caption  = "Source: PhillyStat360 RF model | OPA + L&I administrative data\nZip boundaries: US Census ZCTA 2020 via tigris"
    ) +
    theme_void(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(color = "gray40"),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  p_map_static
  ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "spatial_rf_prob_by_zip_v3.png"),
         plot = p_map_static, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
} else {
  cat("Spatial map skipped — ZCTA boundaries could not be loaded.\n")
}

3d. Faceted Summary: Mean P(vacant) by Zip × Category

top_cats <- type_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(n_parcels)) %>%
  head(4) %>%
  pull(category_code_description)

faceted_zip_cat <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(category_code_description %in% top_cats,
         !is.na(zip_code)) %>%
  group_by(zip_code, category_code_description) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels = n(),
    mean_prob = mean(rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_parcels >= 50)

p_facet <- faceted_zip_cat %>%
  mutate(zip_code = fct_reorder(as.character(zip_code), mean_prob)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_prob, y = zip_code, fill = category_code_description)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ category_code_description, scales = "free_x", nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(0.1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Mean Predicted P(vacant) by Zip Code × Property Category",
    subtitle = "Only zip codes with ≥50 parcels per category | RF model",
    x = "Mean P(vacant)", y = "Zip Code"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

p_facet

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "faceted_zip_category_v3.png"),
       plot = p_facet, width = 13, height = 9, dpi = 300)

4. Interactive Map (Webpage Example)

if (exists("zcta_sf") && !is.null(zcta_sf) && nrow(zcta_sf) > 0) {
  map_data <- zcta_sf %>%
    filter(!is.na(mean_pred_prob)) %>%
    st_transform(4326) %>%
    mutate(
      popup_text = paste0(
        "<b>Zip Code: ", ZCTA5CE20, "</b><br>",
        "Mean P(vacant): ",  percent(mean_pred_prob, 0.01), "<br>",
        "N Parcels: ",       comma(n_parcels),              "<br>",
        "Observed OVS=1: ",  comma(n_obs_vacant), " (",
                             percent(obs_rate, 0.1), ")"
      )
    )

  pal <- colorNumeric(palette = "Blues", domain = map_data$mean_pred_prob)

  vacancy_map <- leaflet(map_data) %>%
    addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>%
    addPolygons(
      fillColor   = ~ pal(mean_pred_prob),
      fillOpacity = 0.75,
      color       = "white",
      weight      = 1,
      popup       = ~ popup_text,
      highlight   = highlightOptions(
        weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
      )
    ) %>%
    addLegend(
      pal       = pal,
      values    = ~ mean_pred_prob,
      title     = "Mean P(vacant)",
      labFormat = labelFormat(transform = function(x) round(x * 100, 2),
                              suffix = "%"),
      position  = "bottomright"
    ) %>%
    addControl(
      html     = "<b>PhillyStat360 Vacancy Risk Model</b><br>
                  <small>Click a zip code for details</small>",
      position = "topright"
    )

  vacancy_map

  saveWidget(vacancy_map,
             file = paste0(graph_path, "vacancy_risk_map_v3.html"),
             selfcontained = TRUE)
  cat("Interactive map saved: graphs/vacancy_risk_map_v3.html\n")
} else {
  cat("Interactive map skipped — ZCTA boundaries not loaded.\n")
}
## Interactive map saved: graphs/vacancy_risk_map_v3.html

4a. Embedding in a Webpage

<!-- PhillyStat360 Vacancy Risk Map -->
<iframe
  src="vacancy_risk_map_v3.html"
  width="100%"
  height="600px"
  style="border:none; border-radius:8px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.15);">
</iframe>

5. Capacity-Based Threshold Selection

Operations teams need a binary decision rule to schedule inspections, but the right threshold depends entirely on how many inspections they can conduct. This section answers: “If we can inspect N properties, what probability cutoff captures the most true vacants while minimising wasted trips?”

# Build a precision/recall/flagged-count table across thresholds
# Restrict to parcels with ground-truth OVS labels (test set or all observed)
threshold_df <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(ovs)) %>%
  select(ovs, rf_prob)

thresholds_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.60, by = 0.01)

capacity_tbl <- map_dfr(thresholds_seq, function(t) {
  flagged   <- threshold_df %>% filter(rf_prob >= t)
  n_flagged <- nrow(flagged)
  n_tp      <- sum(flagged$ovs == 1)
  n_total_p <- sum(threshold_df$ovs == 1)
  tibble(
    threshold  = t,
    n_flagged  = n_flagged,
    precision  = if_else(n_flagged > 0, n_tp / n_flagged, NA_real_),
    recall     = if_else(n_total_p > 0, n_tp / n_total_p, NA_real_)
  )
})

p_capacity <- capacity_tbl %>%
  pivot_longer(c(precision, recall), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_flagged, y = value, color = metric)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma, name = "Parcels Flagged (Inspection Capacity)") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(1), name = NULL, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(precision = "steelblue", recall = "tomato"),
    labels = c(precision = "Precision (% flagged that are truly vacant)",
               recall    = "Recall (% of true vacants caught)")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Capacity-Based Threshold: Precision vs. Recall by Inspection Volume",
    subtitle = "Each point on the curve corresponds to a probability threshold; move right = lower threshold = more inspections",
    color    = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_capacity

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "capacity_threshold_curve.png"),
       plot = p_capacity, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)
# Capacity lookup table: for common inspection volumes, show recommended threshold
capacity_targets <- c(100, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000)

capacity_lookup <- map_dfr(capacity_targets, function(cap) {
  # Find the threshold that flags closest to `cap` parcels from above
  row <- capacity_tbl %>%
    filter(n_flagged >= cap) %>%
    slice_min(n_flagged, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
  if (nrow(row) == 0) row <- capacity_tbl %>% slice_min(threshold, n = 1)
  row %>% mutate(target_capacity = cap)
}) %>%
  select(target_capacity, n_flagged, threshold, precision, recall)

capacity_lookup %>%
  mutate(
    threshold = round(threshold, 3),
    precision = percent(precision, 0.1),
    recall    = percent(recall,    0.1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Target Inspections", "Actual Flagged",
                  "Threshold", "Precision", "Recall"),
    caption   = "Recommended Probability Thresholds by Inspection Capacity"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
Recommended Probability Thresholds by Inspection Capacity
Target Inspections Actual Flagged Threshold Precision Recall
100 1146 0.60 87.2% 16.8%
250 1146 0.60 87.2% 16.8%
500 1146 0.60 87.2% 16.8%
1000 1146 0.60 87.2% 16.8%
2000 2046 0.39 81.4% 28.1%
5000 5156 0.06 61.9% 53.7%

6. Summary Export

zip_summary %>%
  write_csv(paste0(feat_path, "output_zip_summary_v3.csv"))
cat("Exported: output_zip_summary_v3.csv\n")
## Exported: output_zip_summary_v3.csv
type_summary %>%
  write_csv(paste0(feat_path, "output_category_summary_v3.csv"))
cat("Exported: output_category_summary_v3.csv\n")
## Exported: output_category_summary_v3.csv
cat("\n--- Output Analysis v3 Summary ---\n")
## 
## --- Output Analysis v3 Summary ---
cat("Parcels analyzed:", comma(nrow(analysis_df)), "\n")
## Parcels analyzed: 520,196
cat("Zip codes covered:", n_distinct(analysis_df$zip_code, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Zip codes covered: 47
cat("Property categories:",
    n_distinct(analysis_df$category_code_description, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Property categories: 4
cat("rf_prob > 0 (non-zero predictions):",
    percent(mean(analysis_df$rf_prob > 0, na.rm = TRUE), 0.1), "\n")
## rf_prob > 0 (non-zero predictions): 2.9%
cat("Overall mean P(vacant):",
    percent(mean(analysis_df$rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE), 0.01), "\n")
## Overall mean P(vacant): 0.40%
# Load PWD parcel boundaries and join predictions + all features.
# PWD_PARCELS.geojson is 421 MB — expect ~30–60 s to load.
# Output goes to F: drive (outside Box) to avoid sync conflicts on large writes.
cat("Loading PWD_PARCELS.geojson …\n")
## Loading PWD_PARCELS.geojson …
parcels_sf <- st_read(paste0(raw_path, "PWD_PARCELS.geojson"), quiet = TRUE) %>%
  clean_names()

# BRT_ID is the OPA parcel number in PWD data. Stop early with a clear message
# if the column name differs so the user knows what to rename it to.
if (!"brt_id" %in% names(parcels_sf)) {
  stop("Expected column 'brt_id' not found in PWD_PARCELS.geojson. ",
       "Available columns: ", paste(names(parcels_sf), collapse = ", "))
}

parcels_sf <- parcels_sf %>%
  select(parcel_number = brt_id, geometry)

# Predictions + key identifiers only (no raw feature columns)
geojson_df <- preds_df %>%
  select(parcel_number, ovs, data_split,
         rf_prob, logit_prob, xgb_prob,
         rf_flag, logit_flag, xgb_flag, best_model) %>%
  left_join(
    ovs_meta %>% select(parcel_number, zip_code, census_tract,
                        zoning, category_code, category_code_description),
    by = "parcel_number"
  )

output_sf <- parcels_sf %>%
  inner_join(geojson_df, by = "parcel_number") %>%
  st_transform(4326)   # WGS84 — standard for GeoJSON

geojson_out <- paste0(out_path, "vacancy_predictions.geojson")
st_write(output_sf, geojson_out, driver = "GeoJSON", delete_dsn = TRUE)
## Deleting source `F:/PhillyStat360_targets/data/vacancy_predictions.geojson' failed
## Writing layer `vacancy_predictions' to data source 
##   `F:/PhillyStat360_targets/data/vacancy_predictions.geojson' using driver `GeoJSON'
## Writing 485906 features with 15 fields and geometry type Multi Polygon.
cat("Exported: vacancy_predictions.geojson —",
    comma(nrow(output_sf)), "parcels |",
    round(file.size(geojson_out) / 1e6, 1), "MB\n")
## Exported: vacancy_predictions.geojson — 485,906 parcels | 380.8 MB
